title: The two-point trapezoidal rule commutes with scalar multiplication

source.citation: Alfio Quarteroni, Riccardo Sacco, and Fausto Saleri, Numerical Mathematics, 2nd ed., Springer, 2007, Ch. 9 (Numerical Integration).
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The one-cell trapezoidal rule applied to scalar-multiplied endpoint samples equals the scalar multiple of the trapezoidal-rule value.
assumptions: endpoint samples f0 and f1 are given

declarations.name: trapezoid2
declarations.statement: def trapezoid2 (h f0 f1 : Real) : Real := h * (f0 + f1) / 2

declarations.name: trapezoid2_smul
declarations.statement: theorem trapezoid2_smul (h c f0 f1 : Real) : trapezoid2 h (c * f0) (c * f1) = c * trapezoid2 h f0 f1

tasks.input: Translate the quadrature theorem `trapezoid2_smul` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem trapezoid2_smul (h c f0 f1 : Real) : trapezoid2 h (c * f0) (c * f1) = c * trapezoid2 h f0 f1

tasks.input: Identify the main algebraic or order lemma needed for the quadrature proof.
